author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VII. 40–53; VIII. 1–11.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You remember, my beloved, that in the last discourse, by occasion of the passage of the Gospel read, we spoke to you concerning the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the Lord had invited those that believe on Him to this drinking, speaking among those who meditated to lay hold of Him, and sought to kill Him, and were not able, because it was not His will: well, when He had spoken these things, there arose a dissension among the multitude concerning Him; some thinking that He was the very Christ, others saying that Christ shall not arise from Galilee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they who had been sent to take Him returned clear of the crime and full of admiration.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they even gave witness to His divine doctrine, when those by whom they had been sent asked, “Why have ye not brought him?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They answered that they had never heard a man so speak: “For not any man so speaks.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He spake thus, because He was God and man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Pharisees, repelling their testimony, said to them: “Are ye also deceived?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We see, indeed, that you also have been charmed by his discourses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Hath any one of the rulers or the Pharisees believed on him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this multitude who know not the law are cursed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who knew not the law believed on Him who had sent the law; and those men who were teaching the law despised Him, that it might be fulfilled which the Lord Himself had said, “I am come that they who see not may see, and they that see may be made blind.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Pharisees, the teachers of the law, were made blind, and the people that knew not the law, and yet believed on the author of the law, were enlightened.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Nicodemus,” however, “one of the Pharisees, who had come to the Lord by night,”—not indeed as being himself unbelieving, but timid; for therefore he came by night to the light, because he wished to be enlightened and feared to be known;—Nicodemus, I say, answered the Jews, “Doth our law judge a man before it hear him, and know what he doeth?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they perversely wished to condemn before they examined.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nicodemus indeed knew, or rather believed, that if only they were willing to give Him a patient hearing, they would perhaps become like those who were sent to take Him, but preferred to believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They answered, from the prejudice of their heart, what they had answered to those officers, “Art thou also a Galilean?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is, one seduced as it were by the Galilean.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord was said to be a Galilean, because His parents were from the city of Nazareth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have said “His parents” in regard to Mary, not as regards the seed of man; for on earth He sought but a mother, He had already a Father on high.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For His nativity on both sides was marvellous: divine without mother, human without father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, said those would-be doctors of the law to Nicodemus?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Search the Scriptures, and see that out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet the Lord of the prophets arose thence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They returned,” saith the evangelist, “every man to his own house.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Thence Jesus went unto the mount;” namely, to mount “Olivet,”—unto the fruitful mount, unto the mount of ointment, unto the mount of chrism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For where, indeed, but on mount Olivet did it become the Christ to teach?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the name of Christ is from chrism; χρισμα in the Greek, is called in Latin unctio, an anointing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He has anointed us for this reason, because He has made us wrestlers against the devil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And early in the morning He came again into the temple, and all the people came unto Him; and He sat down and taught them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He was not taken, for He did not yet deign to suffer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now observe wherein the Lord’s gentleness was tempted by His enemies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And the scribes and Pharisees brought to Him a woman just taken in adultery: and they set her in the midst, and said to Him, Master, this woman has just been taken in adultery.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now Moses in the law commanded us, that such should be stoned: but what sayest thou?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this they said, tempting Him, that they might accuse Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why accuse Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Had they detected Himself in any misdeed; or was that woman said to have been concerned with Him in any manner?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, is the meaning of “tempting Him, that they might accuse Him”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We understand, brethren, that a wonderful gentleness shone out pre-eminently in the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They observed that He was very meek, very gentle: for of Him it had been previously foretold, “Gird Thy sword upon Thy thigh, O most Mighty; in Thy splendor and beauty urge on, march on prosperously, and reign, because of truth, and meekness, and righteousness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, as a teacher, He brought truth; as a deliverer, He brought gentleness; as a protector, He brought righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That He was to reign on account of these things, the prophet had by the Holy Spirit foretold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When He spoke His truth was acknowledged; when He was not provoked to anger against His enemies, His meekness was praised.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whilst, therefore, in respect of these two,—namely, His truth and meekness,—His enemies were tormented with malice and envy; in respect of the third,—namely, righteousness,—they laid a stumbling-block for Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what way?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because the law had commanded the adulterers to be stoned, and surely the law could not command what was unjust: if any man should say other than the law had commanded, he would be detected as unjust.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore they said among themselves, “He is accounted true, he appears to be gentle; an accusation must be sought against him in respect of righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us bring before him a woman taken in adultery; let us say to him what is ordered in the law concerning such: if he shall approve her being stoned, he will not show his gentleness; if he consent to let her go, he will not keep righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, say they, that he may not lose the reputation of gentleness, for which he is become an object of love to the people, without doubt he will say that she must be let go.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence we find an opportunity of accusing him, and we charge him as being a transgressor of the law: saying to him, Thou art an enemy to the law; thou answerest against Moses, nay, against Him who gave the law through Moses; thou art worthy of death: thou too must be stoned with this woman.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By these words and sentiments they might possibly be able to inflame envy against Him, to urge accusation, and cause His condemnation to be eagerly demanded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this against whom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was perversity against rectitude, falsehood against the truth, the corrupt heart against the upright heart, folly against wisdom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When did such men prepare snares, into which they did not first thrust their own heads?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, the Lord in answering them will both keep righteousness, and will not depart from gentleness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was not taken for whom the snare was laid, but rather they were taken who laid it, because they believed not on Him who could pull them out of the net.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What answer, then, did the Lord Jesus make?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How answered the Truth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How answered Wisdom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How answered that Righteousness against which a false accusation was ready?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not say, Let her not be stoned; lest He should seem to speak against the law.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But God forbid that He should say, Let her be stoned: for He came not to lose what He had found, but to seek what was lost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then did He answer?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See you how full it is of righteousness, how full of meekness and truth!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that is without sin of you,” saith He, “let him first cast a stone at her.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O answer of Wisdom!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How He sent them unto themselves!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For without they stood to accuse and censure, themselves they examined not inwardly: they saw the adulteress, they looked not into themselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Transgressors of the law, they wished the law to be fulfilled, and this by heedlessly accusing; not really fulfilling it, as if condemning adulteries by chastity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have heard, O Jews, you have heard, O Pharisees, you have heard, O teachers of the law, the guardian of the law, but have not yet understood Him as the Lawgiver.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What else does He signify to you when He writes with His finger on the ground?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the law was written with the finger of God; but written on stone because of the hard-hearted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord now wrote on the ground, because He was seeking fruit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have heard then, Let the law be fulfilled, let the adulteress be stoned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But is it by punishing her that the law is to be fulfilled by those that ought to be punished?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let each of you consider himself, let him enter into himself, ascend the judgment-seat of his own mind, place himself at the bar of his own conscience, oblige himself to confess.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he knows what he is: for “no man knoweth the things of a man, but the spirit of man which is in him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Each looking carefully into himself, finds himself a sinner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yes, indeed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, either let this woman go, or together with her receive ye the penalty of the law.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Had He said, Let not the adulteress be stoned, He would be proved unjust: had He said, Let her be stoned, He would not appear gentle: let Him say what it became Him to say, both the gentle and the just, “Whoso is without sin of you, let him first cast a stone at her.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the voice of Justice: Let her, the sinner, be punished, but not by sinners: let the law be fulfilled, but not by the transgressors of the law.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This certainly is the voice of justice: by which justice, those men pierced through as if by a dart, looking into themselves and finding themselves guilty, “one after another all withdrew.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The two were left alone, the wretched woman and Mercy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord, having struck them through with that dart of justice, deigned not to heed their fall, but, turning away His look from them, “again He wrote with His finger on the ground.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when that woman was left alone, and all they were gone out, He raised His eyes to the woman.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have heard the voice of justice, let us also hear the voice of clemency.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I suppose that woman was the more terrified when she had heard it said by the Lord, “He that is without sin of you, let him first cast a stone at her.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they, turning their thought to themselves, and by that very withdrawal having confessed concerning themselves, had left the woman with her great sin to Him who was without sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And because she had heard this, “He that is without sin. let him first cast a stone at her,” she expected to be punished by Him in whom sin could not be found.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He, who had driven back her adversaries with the tongue of justice, raising the eyes of clemency towards her, asked her, “Hath no man condemned thee?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She answered, “No man, Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He said, “Neither do I condemn thee;” by whom, perhaps, thou didst fear to be condemned, because in me thou hast not found sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Neither will I condemn thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this, O Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost Thou therefore favor sins?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not so, evidently.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mark what follows: “Go, henceforth sin no more.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the Lord did also condemn, but condemned sins, not man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He were a patron of sin, He would say, Neither will I condemn thee; go, live as thou wilt: be secure in my deliverance; how much soever thou wilt sin, I will deliver thee from all punishment even of hell, and from the tormentors of the infernal world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said not this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them take heed, then, who love His gentleness in the Lord, and let them fear His truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “The Lord is sweet and right.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou lovest Him in that He is sweet; fear Him in that He is right.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As the meek, He said, “I held my peace;” but as the just, He said, “Shall I always be silent?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Lord is merciful and pitiful.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So He is, certainly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Add yet further, “Long-suffering;” add yet further, “And very pitiful:” but fear what comes last, “And true.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For those whom He now bears with as sinners, He will judge as despisers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Or despisest thou the riches of His long-suffering and gentleness; not knowing that the forbearance of God leadeth thee to repentance?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But thou, after thy hardness and impenitent heart, treasurest up for thyself wrath against the day of wrath and the revelation of the righteous judgment of God; who will render to every man according to his deeds.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord is gentle, the Lord is long-suffering, the Lord is pitiful; but the Lord is also just, the Lord is also true.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He bestows on thee space for correction; but thou lovest the delay of judgment more than the amendment of thy ways.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou been a bad man yesterday?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To-day be a good man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou gone on in thy wickedness to-day?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At any rate change to-morrow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art always expecting, and from the mercy of God makest exceeding great promises to thyself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if He, who has promised thee pardon through repentance, promised thee also a longer life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How knowest thou what to-morrow may bring forth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rightly thou sayest in thy heart: When I shall have corrected my ways, God will put all my sins away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We cannot deny that God has promised pardon to those that have amended their ways and are converted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in what prophet thou readest to me that God has promised pardon to him that amends, thou dost not read to me that God has promised thee a long life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From both, then, men are in danger; both from hoping and despairing, from contrary things, from contrary affections.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is deceived by hoping?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who says, God is good, God is merciful, let me do what I please, what I like; let me give loose reins to my lusts, let me gratify the desires of my soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because God is merciful, God is good, God is kind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These men are in danger by hope.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And those are in danger from despair, who, having fallen into grievous sins, fancying that they can no more be pardoned upon repentance, and believing that they are without doubt doomed to damnation, do say with themselves, We are already destined to be damned, why not do what we please with the disposition of gladiators destined to the sword.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the reason that desperate men are dangerous: for, having no longer aught to fear, they are to be feared exceedingly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Despair kills these; hope, those.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The mind is tossed to and fro between hope and despair.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast to fear lest hope slay thee; and, when thou hopest much from mercy, lest thou fall into judgment: again, thou hast to fear lest despair slay thee, and, when thou thinkest that the grievous sins which thou hast committed cannot be forgiven thee, thou dost not repent, and thou incurrest the sentence of Wisdom, which says, “I also will laugh at your perdition.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How then does the Lord treat those who are in danger from both these maladies?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To those who are in danger from hope, He says, “Be not slow to be converted to the Lord, neither put it off from day to day; for suddenly His anger will come, and in the time of vengeance, will utterly destroy thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To those who are in danger from despair, what does He say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In what day soever the wicked man shall be converted, I will forget all his iniquities.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, for the sake of those who are in danger by despair, He has offered us a refuge of pardon; and because of those who are in danger by hope, and are deluded by delays, He has made the day of death uncertain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou knowest not when thy last day may come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Art thou ungrateful because thou hast to-day on which thou mayest be improved?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus therefore said He to the woman, “Neither will I condemn thee;” but, being made secure concerning the past, beware of the future.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Neither will I condemn thee:” I have blotted out what thou hast done; keep what I have commanded thee, that thou mayest find what I have promised.
